content_of_post_to_comment:
I realized most students don’t actually lack motivation.

They lack direction.

You watch a motivational reel, sit to study, open a huge syllabus… and 30 mins later you’re still flipping pages confused.

So I’m building LifePilot — an AI that finds the highest-return topics using the 80/20 principle and tells you what to focus on.

Study smarter, not harder. 🧠
comment: The 80/20 idea makes sense, but I think a lot of students also struggle with knowing what a topic actually means once they've identified it. Finding the highest-return chapters is useful, but turning that into a clear sequence of concepts and practice questions is where people usually get stuck. Curious how LifePilot handles that part.
